note: Alpha-grade LLM eval. 10/20+ cases, 1/3+ LLM families. Full release gate at v0.7.0. Responses generated by claude-sonnet-4-6 using syntax-reference.md as system prompt, validated with strux build (parser v0.6.0). Three parser bugs were discovered and fixed during this run: (1) numeric range constraint lexer ambiguity [0..N], (2) @context block skip consumed LBRACE as name token, (3) captureRawExpr did not track paren/bracket depth.

cases.prompt: Define a `GrantApplication` record type with 6 fields: `id` (string), `title` (string), `amount` (number, 0..1000000), `status` (string), `submittedAt` (date), `applicantId` (string). The record models a grant application entity.
Define a `GrantStatus` enum with 5 variants: `draft`, `submitted`, `under_review`, `approved`, `rejected`. This models the lifecycle states of a grant application.

Define a `ReviewDecision` union with 3 variants: `approved` carrying an `approvedAmount` (number), `rejected` carrying a `reason` (string), and `deferred` carrying a `reviewDate` (date). Model a grant review outcome.

Write a panel named `submit-application` that: receives an HTTP POST to `/applications`, validates the body against a `GrantApplication` schema, stores the result to a Postgres database named `@db`, and responds with the stored record. Use shorthand form.

Write a panel named `approve-application` that only allows users with the `reviewer` or `admin` role to approve a grant application. Receive a PATCH to `/applications/:id/approve`, check role using a guard rod, update the record, and respond. Use shorthand form.
cases.parserFixNote: Required captureRawExpr paren-depth fix to correctly parse HAS ANY ("...", "...") expressions.

cases.prompt: Write a panel named `list-pending-applications` that reads all grant applications where status is `submitted` AND amount is between 10000 and 500000 from a Postgres database named `@db`, and responds with the results. Use filter expression shorthand.

Define a `strux.context` file that sets project-wide data protection defaults (controller "NLnet Foundation", controller_id "NL-2024", dpo "dpo@nlnet.nl"), a named Postgres source `@db`, and `@ops` (retry 3, timeout "30s"). Then write a panel that inherits all of it without repeating config.
cases.parserFixNote: Required @context block skip fix (was consuming LBRACE as name token).

cases.prompt: Write a panel named `sync-to-analytics` that reads grant applications from a production Postgres database named `@production` and writes them to a BigQuery analytics target named `@analytics`, both defined in `strux.context`. Compare to writing the connection config inline in each panel.

Write a panel named `search-applications` that: receives a GET to `/applications/search`, validates query parameters against a `SearchParams` schema, filters applications from `@db` by the query, projects to only `id`, `title`, `status`, and `amount` fields, and responds with the results. Use shorthand 5-rod pipeline.
cases.parserFixNote: Required captureRawExpr bracket-depth fix to parse fields: [id, title, status, amount] arrays.

cases.prompt: Write a panel named `export-for-audit` that reads grant applications from `@db`, pseudonymizes the `applicantId` field using SHA-256, encrypts the `title` and `amount` fields using a key reference named `audit-key`, and writes the result to an archive target `@audit-store`. This models a GDPR-compliant audit export.

summary.parserFixNote: 3 parser bugs found and fixed during eval: range constraint lexer, @context skip, captureRawExpr depth tracking. All B001-B010 parse cleanly after fixes. Scores reflect corrected parser.
summary.scorecardNote: Alpha-only: 10/20+ required cases, 1/3+ required LLM families. Syntax validity rate 100% and construct presence rate 100% for evaluated cases. Full gate at v0.7.0.
